blog_publish: Publishing blog posts to austegard.com or muninn.austegard.com, linking Bluesky engagement widgets to posts, updating Atom feeds.
bsky_card: Posting any link to Bluesky via API with a proper card preview (blog posts, tools, apps, dashboards, etc.)
bsky_limit: Posting to Bluesky and need to verify/enforce the 300-grapheme limit. len() lies on emoji and combining marks.
flowing: You have 3+ sequential tool calls where the workflow shape is known upfront. Also when pipelines need checkpoint resume (fix step N, resume without re-running steps 1 to N-1), or when side-effects (memory storage, notifications) should not block the main pipeline.
issue_close: Closing a GitHub issue and capturing the behavioral lesson learned. Optionally tags the synthesis memory as a pending test for the next session.
memory_tfidf: therapy Phase 1 duplicate detection, Phase 2 structural pattern matching, memory deduplication, finding related memories across domains
perch_publish: Publishing flight log discussions to the public perch section of muninn.austegard.com.
perch_triage: Dream review, morning check-in, or any flight log processing.
remind: Oskar says "remind me", "don't let me forget", scheduling future reminders, checking/managing existing reminders.
verify_patch: PR creation, code review, verifying handoff specs, any time a diff needs vetting
whtwnd: publishing blog posts to WhiteWind — whtwnd_auth(); whtwnd_post(content, title, auth=); whtwnd_update(rkey, content, title, auth=); whtwnd_delete(rkey, auth=); whtwnd_list(auth=); whtwnd_upload_image(path, auth=)
zeitgeist_delta: Running a zeitgeist and about to store the result. Prevents near-identical coverage of running stories (Iran/Hormuz, Norway F-16s) from bloating memory.
recall_sufficiency: Iterative recall that keeps searching until the answer is sufficient: recall -> judge coverage -> name the gap and re-search for it -> repeat. For autonomous/programmatic runs or cross-corpus retrieval where bailing at 'not found' is wrong; on a single rich KB plain recall() plus judgement is lighter.
github_rw: Branch-aware GitHub writes from a spoke / career-search / any-repo workflow: commit a file to a branch, create a branch, open a PR, check live PR state. The write companion to gh_status; stops hand-rolling urllib contents-PUT + pulls boilerplate.
correction_gate: A therapy correction is about to become boot-loaded context (new desire-trigger, ops entry). Held-in + held-out regression gate: does the correction catch the failure that motivated it, and does it regress nothing else (a trigger firing on an unrelated past input, boot bloat)? Gates only the three measurable slices — trigger-firing, recall precision, reindex; voice/relevance stay hand-evolved. Auto-runs from set_rule.
satisfaction_skew: Auditing the failure:success storage skew — how many correction-tagged entries there are per satisfaction-analog, its monthly trend, and the shape distribution of registered analogs. Reach for it when re-evaluating the satisfaction-register (is it under-firing? is a 4th shape emerging?) or answering Weng-style questions about approach- vs avoidance-orientation in the corpus. Read-only measurement, never fires a trigger.
boot_ledger: Auditing or pruning the boot payload: which boot-loaded triggers/ops/voice-signature entries cost the most tokens per unit of actual use, what to demote to reference-only, whether a collapse dropped boot cost. Ranks every boot_load=1 config entry by exact token cost vs a memory-corpus fire-rate proxy; ships an opt-in config_get fire counter (MUNINN_INSTRUMENT_FIRES) for exact go-forward data. Companion to correction_gate (one guards a write, this measures the whole payload).
bsky_moderation: Moderating a Bluesky thread's repliers in bulk: extract DIDs+text of everyone who replied to a post (extract_thread_repliers, context-lean), classify them, then mute/block the matching accounts (moderate, bounded-parallel, dry_run-default). Two-stage: extraction + authed action, classification is the caller's job.
